filename: RFQ for IAR and AGM Notice.pdf

scope_of_work.description: Comprehensive development of Integrated Annual Report for FY 2023-24 including content development, design, typesetting in English and Hindi, and BRSR preparation aligned with IIRC Framework
deliverables: Content development for six capitals structure
Writing narrative sections including Chairman's message, KPIs, value creation story
English and Hindi versions of IAR

print_specs.paper_gsm: Cover: 300 gsm Art card, Corporate overview: 170 gsm Art Paper, Statutory: 70 gsm Maplitho
print_specs.other: Cover with matte lamination, spot UV, foil stamping for logo. Multi-color corporate overview, two-color statutory part

digital_deliverables: Web-ready PDF with navigation and bookmarks

frameworks.framework_notes: Explicit requirement to align with International Integrated Reporting Council (IIRC) Framework and six capitals structure. BRSR mentioned for preparation and gap analysis.

key_dates.notes: Subject to C&AG comments and OL Dept clearance

evaluation_criteria.method: Not specified - based on technical presentation

financial_terms.payment_structure: Milestone-based: 35% on first acceptable draft, 65% on final submission
financial_terms.gst_treatment: GST applicable - separate column in price bid

special_requirements: 10% page increase accommodation without additional cost
Team lead must be fully committed and available for all meetings
Possible visits to Bengaluru corporate office
LD clause applicable as per BEL standard terms

clarifications.question: The timeline shows Final English IAR due by July 15, 2024, but this appears to be for FY 2023-24 which would have ended March 31, 2024. Given that we're likely past these dates, what are the revised timelines for all deliverables including draft submissions, final reports, and AGM notice?
clarifications.reason: The entire project timeline appears outdated which will impact resource planning, proposal costing, and delivery commitments

clarifications.question: While IIRC Framework is mentioned, as a listed PSU in defense manufacturing, BEL must also comply with BRSR mandatory disclosures. What is the expected scope of BRSR integration within the IAR - Essential Indicators only or Leadership Indicators as well? Should TCFD climate disclosures also be included given the defense sector's increasing focus on climate risks?
clarifications.reason: BRSR compliance scope directly impacts content development effort, data collection requirements, and assurance activities

clarifications.question: The scope mentions 'assisting with assurance' for BRSR (Activity 23). Does this mean BEL has already appointed an assurance provider, or are we expected to coordinate assurance procurement? What level of assurance is required (limited or reasonable) and which specific disclosures need external assurance?
clarifications.reason: Assurance coordination significantly impacts project timeline, stakeholder management, and potential additional costs

clarifications.question: Page counts are specified as Corporate Overview 45-55 pages and Statutory part ~320 pages, with 10% flexibility on statutory pages. However, what is the total expected page count for the complete IAR? Are there specific page allocations for the six capitals discussion, materiality assessment, and value creation sections?
clarifications.reason: Total page count impacts design complexity, content development effort, printing costs, and project pricing

clarifications.question: Content inputs are mentioned to be provided in May 2024 (statutory reports) and June 2024 (Chairman's letter, AGM notice). Given the apparent timeline delays, what is the current status of content readiness? Are financial statements finalized, and has the Board's Report including MD&A been drafted?
clarifications.reason: Content readiness directly impacts our ability to meet any revised timelines and affects resource allocation planning

clarifications.question: Activity 7 mentions conducting materiality assessment including stakeholder identification. Has BEL conducted any materiality assessment previously, or will this be developed from scratch? What is the expected methodology (surveys, interviews, workshops) and how many stakeholder groups should be covered?
clarifications.reason: Materiality assessment scope significantly impacts project timeline, stakeholder coordination effort, and methodology costs

clarifications.question: The RFQ mentions fortnightly progress meetings (Activity 18) and team lead availability for all management meetings. What is the expected frequency of stakeholder meetings beyond fortnightly reviews? How many site visits to Bengaluru are anticipated, and should travel/accommodation costs be included in our quote despite point 1 in price conditions?
clarifications.reason: There's a contradiction between extensive meeting requirements and exclusion of travel costs, which needs clarification for accurate pricing

clarifications.question: Activity 1 requires conducting training workshops on IIRC Framework for stakeholders. How many participants are expected, how many sessions, and what is the preferred format (in-person in Bengaluru, virtual, or hybrid)? Should training materials and documentation be provided post-training?
clarifications.reason: Training scope affects resource allocation, travel requirements, and deliverable specifications

clarifications.question: Multiple design options are required (10 cover options, 5 corporate overview layout options, 5-7 template design options). What is the expected approval process for these options - will BEL select from presented options or will there be iterative refinement rounds? How many revision rounds are included for each design element?
clarifications.reason: Design approval process and revision rounds significantly impact project timeline and design resource requirements

clarifications.question: The printing specifications mention specific paper GSM (300 gsm cover, 170 gsm corporate overview, 70 gsm statutory) and finishing (matte lamination, spot UV, foil stamping). Are there any sustainability requirements for paper sourcing (FSC certified, recycled content) that should be specified in our print-ready files?
clarifications.reason: Sustainability requirements for printing materials may be important for a PSU and could affect printer coordination

clarifications.question: Who are the specific stakeholders in the approval chain for content and design sign-offs? What is the typical approval cycle time for each milestone, and are there backup approvers if primary stakeholders are unavailable during critical approval periods?
clarifications.reason: Understanding approval hierarchy and cycle times is crucial for realistic timeline planning and risk management

clarifications.question: Activity 6 mentions cross-functional teams and senior management interactions for data collection. Which specific departments/functions need to be engaged (Finance, Operations, HR, Legal, IT, Manufacturing units), and what is the expected time commitment from each department for data collection and validation?
clarifications.reason: Understanding departmental involvement helps plan stakeholder coordination effort and potential delays in data collection

results.proposal:
Proposal for Bharat Electronics Limited / Integrated Annual Report FY 2023-24 | 15th March 2024

Dear Sir/Madam,

We truly appreciate the interest shown by you in WP Communications for working on Bharat Electronics Limited Integrated Annual Report FY 2023-24. We are pleased to give you our best competitive proposal along with other details.

SCOPE OF WORK:

• Conduct comprehensive training sessions and workshops for BEL's stakeholders and core team on IIRC Framework and Integrated Reporting process, including framework implementation guidance

• Review previous year's Integrated Annual Reports of BEL and conduct detailed gap analysis against International Integrated Reporting Council (IIRC) framework, presenting findings with recommended improvements and changes to the Committee

• Develop and present at least 10 theme options for the Integrated Annual Report, highlighting BEL's vision, growth trajectory, performance context, and business landscape with detailed rationale for each theme

• Create comprehensive Integrated Annual Report structure aligned with IIRC Framework, developing content for six capitals structure based on IR guiding principles and latest framework content elements

• Design pre-defined questionnaires and templates for data collection, conduct cross-functional meetings with core team and senior management to gather performance data and strategic insights

• Conduct materiality assessment identifying key stakeholders and material issues, mapping external environment and company responses aligned with MD&A disclosures

• Write and develop narrative sections including about this report, index, at a glance sections, key metrics, geographical presence, Chairman's message, KPIs, six capitals interlinkage, value creation story, theme stories, governance framework, and megatrends analysis

• Develop each section following IIRC Framework guiding principles and content elements, providing structural guidance for risk management model and corporate governance practices

• Content development with storytelling and copywriting ensuring uniformity of tone, tenor and style across the report, maintaining data consistency without duplication

• Edit and enhance MD&A content provided by BEL based on interactions and research

• Design cover pages with at least 10 creative options based on finalized theme, including rationale for theme, tagline and cover design for each option

• Design Corporate Overview section (estimated pages: 45-55) with multi-color creative layouts including vision & mission, financial highlights, geographic footprint, business segments, stakeholder engagement, six capitals discussion, Board profiles, CSR initiatives with at least 5 design options

• Typesetting and template design for Statutory Part (estimated pages: 320) in double-color format with running text, tables, graphs and financial statements, providing 5-7 layout options with accommodation for 10% page increase at no additional cost

• Complete typesetting, artwork and DTP for entire Integrated Annual Report in English & Hindi languages and Notice of AGM in bilingual format

• Grammar check, editing, value addition and visualization for Corporate Overview, Chairman's Letter, Board's Report, MD&A, Corporate Governance Report, BRSR and AGM Notice

• Standardization and proofreading of entire report ensuring style uniformity, language accuracy, design consistency and cross-report information linkages

• Attend fortnightly progress review meetings with Project Head and team members as scheduled by BEL

• Develop graphs, tables, charts and infographics as required for enhanced data presentation

• Prepare, review and conduct gap analysis of Business Responsibility & Sustainability Report including core mapping and assurance assistance

• Provide guidance and specifications for photography requirements for IAR

• Deliver print-ready PDFs for offset/digital printing with maximum quality specifications (Cover: 300 gsm Art card with matte lamination, spot UV, foil stamping; Corporate overview: 170 gsm Art Paper; Statutory: 70 gsm Maplitho; Perfect binding with creasing)

• Deliver web-ready PDFs with navigation, bookmarks and cross-reference hyperlinks for digital sharing

• Provide complete source files for English IAR, Hindi IAR and bilingual AGM Notice for printer coordination

PLEASE NOTE:
• Cost excludes taxes as levied by government from time to time
• Cost excludes outstation travel (air travel, road travel) and accommodation by WP representative beyond the initial pre project commencement meeting
• Cost excludes image purchase cost from royalty free sites and photography costs
• Layout/art working of the entire document will be in publishing software (InDesign)
• Re-layout of statutory and financial pages will be charged at Rs. 650 + taxes per page
• Cost of additional theme/non-statutory section pages will be charged at Rs. 7,500 + taxes per page and Statutory financial pages will be charged at Rs. 1,750 + taxes per page
• 10% increase in number of pages will be accommodated without additional cost as per RFQ requirement
• If the project is aborted at any stage for no fault of the agency, 85% of the total amount shall be payable to WP, with the remaining 15% being waived off
• In case, there is a change of brief provided by the client, post approval of text and design approach, an additional cost ranging from 10-50% of the total cost would be levied, depending on the stage at which the brief is changed
• Single point of contact will be designated for all correspondence
• Team lead will be fully committed and available for all meetings as required
• We require a written approval letter and a purchase order of acceptance before we commence the project work
• This proposal is valid for next 30 days only

TIMELINE:
Project timeline: Estimated 5 months from the date of commencement, with key milestones aligned to BEL's specified dates including draft submission by 30 days, content finalization by 60 days, and final deliverables by August 2024

COSTING:
WP Communications charges for the above scope of work will be Rs. [To be discussed] + applicable taxes.

PAYMENT TERMS:
• Formal Purchase order on approval & 35% payment on submission of first acceptable draft of complete Integrated Annual Report
• Balance 65% payment after submission of final Integrated Annual Report (English & Hindi) and AGM Notice with source files

BENEFITS:
• Working with a specialized Top-notch Financial & Corporate Communications agency
• Providing a one-stop solution for all your requirements
• A designated senior "Project Head" to service the account
• Agreed timeline to execute the projects
• Well defined procedures, suggested time schedules, SOPs and constant interaction to ensure minimum last minute hurdles

WP assures you of best service at all times. We trust you would find the above to your satisfaction. Should you desire any further information, please feel free to contact us.

Sincerely,
CA Shailesh Vyas,
Director (Marketing)
+91 98200 67242
